title: Bridge Paper: Imageless imagery in aphantasia revealed by early visual cortex decoding.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Mental imagery — visual imagery as real perception, imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.
**Phase context:** mental imagery, visual imagery, imagination, aphantasia, mental simulation, pareidolia

Paper: Imageless imagery in aphantasia revealed by early visual cortex decoding.
DOI: 10.1016/j.cub.2024.12.012
Abstract: Activity in the early visual cortex is thought to tightly couple with conscious experience, including feedback-driven mental imagery. However, in aphantasia (a complete lack of visual imagery), the state of mental imagery, what takes its place, or how any activity relates to qualia remains unknown. This study analyzed univariate (amplitude) and multivariate (decoding) blood-oxygen-level-dependent (BOLD) signals in primary visual cortex during imagery attempts. "Imagery" content could be decoded

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-11. Phase 10 (Imaginal Reconstruction).
